abstract: --idea theory admits a clean mathematical axiomatization. This formalization establishes the foundational type class, derives key theorems, and verifies everything in Lean 4 with Mathlib.

tasks.title: Lean toolchain and lakefile
tasks.description: Create lean-toolchain (Lean 4.15.0) and lean/lakefile.lean requiring Mathlib.

tasks.title: Lean 4 foundations
tasks.description: Create lean/IdeaTheory/Foundations.lean. Define type class IdeaTheoryStructure with axioms A1–A3. Prove foundational theorems. Zero sorries. Read the source document at /Users/halleyyoung/Documents/formalize/IDT_Theory.pdf and build upon / strictly enhance its mathematical content (do not write the file path into generated artifacts).

tasks.title: Lean theorems: Idea Structures
tasks.description: Create lean/IdeaTheory/Theorems1.lean for 'Idea Structures'. Import Foundations. Prove theorems 1.1, 1.2, 1.3. Zero sorries. Make them substantial theorems which use tens to hundreds of helper lemmas.  They can also import any of the other lean files in the lean folder.  Write incrementally to disk after every two lemmas/theorems, lean compile, and continue.  CORE REQUIREMENT — literature bridge: each top-level theorem MUST carry a Lean docstring (`/-- … -/`) that (a) names the informal claim from the existing non-formalized literature on '--idea theory' that this theorem formalizes, (b) cites the source (Author, Year, work), and (c) notes any place the formal statement sharpens, generalizes, or contradicts the informal version. Volume 1 context.

tasks.title: Website CSS
tasks.description: Create docs/style.css dark GitHub-Pages style theme.

tasks.title: Website index + navigation pages
tasks.description: Create all navigation pages for IdeaTheory. Every page must have <nav> linking all other pages.

tasks.title: Website page: Idea Structures
tasks.description:
Create docs/idea-structures.html for 'Idea Structures'. Cross-link to ALL other concept pages, axioms.html, theorems.html, literature.html, and the corresponding Lean source.  REQUIRED structure (in order):
  1. Definition box.
  2. 'In the Literature' — 150–300 word substantive narrative on what the existing non-formalized literature on '--idea theory' says about Idea Structures: which authors introduced it, competing definitions, central informal claims, open questions, folklore.  Reference 3–6 named works.
  3. Three theorem boxes with ✓ verified badges; each cites the informal claim it formalizes.
  4. 'Bridge: Informal Claim → Formal Theorem' — a table whose rows are (Informal claim, Formal theorem, Source).
  5. 'Further Reading' — bulleted list of 4+ real references.

tasks.title: Website: literature bridge index
tasks.description:
Create docs/literature.html: the master index connecting the existing non-formalized literature on '--idea theory' to the machine-verified theorems in this project.  REQUIRED:
  * Narrative introduction (200–400 words) on why bridging matters.
  * 'Why Bridge?' section: which long-standing claims become testable, which definitions sharpen, where the literature disagrees.
  * Long cross-walk table: rows are informal sources/claims; columns are (Author/Year, Informal claim, Formal theorem, Lean file, Concept page).
  * 'References, Grouped by Sub-Topic' section (Foundational works, Recent developments, Surveys).
  * Sidebar/topbar navigation must link this page from every other page; this page must link back to all concept pages, axioms.html, theorems.html.

tasks.title: TeX preamble
tasks.description: Create tex/preamble.sty for IdeaTheory.

tasks.title: BibTeX bibliography
tasks.description:
Create tex/references.bib with 15–40 REAL bibliography entries.  REQUIRED:
  * Lean 4 / Mathlib core references (Theorem Proving in Lean 4, Mathlib).
  * 12+ real domain references for '--idea theory' — actual authors, real years, real titles of published works (papers, books, monographs, theses).  No invented citations.  These are the works the chapters and concept pages will cite when bridging informal literature to formal theorems.

tasks.title: TeX ch (V1): Foundations and Axioms
tasks.description:
Create tex/vol1/ch.tex for 'Foundations and Axioms' (Volume 1: Foundations). REQUIRED 7-section structure (in order):
  \section{Background from the Literature} — 300–600 word narrative on what existing non-formalized writing on '--idea theory' says about Foundations and Axioms; cite 4–8 real works from references.bib.
  \section{From Informal Claims to Formal Statements} — translation table mapping informal claims → Lean theorems with citations.
  \section{Definitions} — formal definitions, each citing its informal antecedent.
  \section{Main Results} — theorem+proof environments.
  \section{Lean 4 Verification} — lstlisting with the actual Lean theorem names.
  \section{Discussion: What the Formalization Reveals} — reflect on what the formal proofs sharpen, simplify, or refute about the literature.
  \section{Notes and References}.
Use \cref cross-references throughout.

Use \cref cross-references throughout.

  \section{Main Results} — theorem+proof environments.
  \section{Lean 4 Verification} — lstlisting with the actual Lean theorem names.
  \section{Discussion: What the Formalization Reveals} — reflect on what the formal proofs sharpen, simplify, or refute about the literature.
  \section{Notes and References}.
Use \cref cross-references throughout.
